target.name: Xsight Labs
target.founder: Guy Koren, Erez Shaizaf, Gal Malach

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $281M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Xsight Labs is a Series D chip design company in the Semiconductors sector that has raised $281M to date. The private peer set comprises 8 comparable companies at similar or adjacent stages, anchored by three public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 24.2x.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring on total capital raised as a proxy, applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier from Series D forward, yielding $422M. Peer-median analysis draws from three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—SambaNova Systems at $5.0B, Tenstorrent at $2.7B, and DEEPX at $529M—producing a median implied value of $2.7B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied as the target's revenue is not disclosed. The triangulation across these two methods suggests a valuation range of $358M (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $1.6B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion across peer valuations ($529M to $5.0B) and the absence of revenue data to validate public-market multiples. Stage-progression implies material upside relative to capital deployed, while peer medians suggest values materially above capital raised, reflecting investor conviction in the sector's growth trajectory.
